description_long: This object is a cylindrical hand grenade, specifically modeled after the M84 stun grenade, commonly known as a flashbang. The body is olive drab with a series of circular cutouts along its length, revealing a metallic inner chamber, and is banded with a teal-green stripe. Both ends are hexagonal, providing stability when placed on surfaces, and the top features a prominent safety lever and pull ring, which are typically used to arm the device. The lever is metallic and painted in a reddish-brown color, contrasting with the rest of the grenade. Small white alphanumeric markings are visible on the upper section, indicating model and lot information. The overall construction suggests a robust, metallic build designed for tactical use in urban environments.
description: A cylindrical, olive drab stun grenade with teal banding, hexagonal ends, and a metallic safety lever and pull ring. Circular cutouts reveal the inner chamber, and white markings are present on the upper body.
description_view_0: The grenade is shown with its safety lever and pull ring clearly visible, highlighting the hexagonal end and teal banding.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the white alphanumeric markings on the upper section and provides a clear look at the circular cutouts and the reddish-brown lever.
description_view_2: The rear of the grenade is visible, focusing on the metallic safety lever and the hexagonal end cap, with less emphasis on the cutouts.
description_view_3: A side angle showing both the cutouts and the lever, with the pull ring and teal banding prominent along the body.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The grenade is armed by pulling the safety ring and releasing the lever, which likely triggers an internal fuse mechanism.
